IDirect3DDevice8::InsertCallback

Inserts a callback into the push buffer that will be executed when the graphics processing unit (GPU) has processed all of the commands in the push buffer up to the point of the callback.

VOID InsertCallback(
  D3DCALLBACKTYPE Type, 
  D3DCALLBACK pCallback, 
  DWORD Context
);

Parameters

Type
[in] Defines when to call the callback. It may be one of the following values:
D3DCALLBACK_READ
Executes the callback when all of the previous commands in the command-stream have finished reading from the resources they are consuming (for example, vertex buffers and textures).
D3DCALLBACK_WRITE
Executes the callback when all of the previous commands have finished writing to the render target. This will flush the GPU pipeline.
pCallback
[in] Specifies the address of a callback function of type D3DCALLBACK that is called when the callback in the push buffer is executed.
Context
[in] A 32-bit value that is passed to the callback. This can be set to any value the title desires. It will be passed as the Context parameter of the callback routine.

Remarks

InsertCallback cannot be called while recording a push buffer (in other words, from within an IDirect3DDevice8::BeginPushBuffer/IDirect3DDevice8::EndPushBuffer block). It is designed to be placed at the end of a push buffer to provide an indication that the push buffer processing is finished.

InsertCallback is called at the time of deferred procedure calls (DPC) and should do little processing. Only a few XTL functions are safe to call at DPC level: The Interlocked functions are safe to use, as are the following XTL functions, if given a valid object handle:

  • SetEvent
  • ResetEvent
  • PulseEvent
  • ReleaseSemaphore
  • ReleaseMutex

When these functions are called at DPC with an invalid handle, their behavior is undefined.

Do not perform floating-point math, SSE instructions, and MMX instructions during DPC unless the XSaveFloatingPointStateForDpc and XRestoreFloatingPointStateForDpc functions are used.

See Also

IDirect3DDevice8::BlockUntilVerticalBlank, IDirect3DDevice8::InsertFence, IDirect3DDevice8::IsFencePending, IDirect3DDevice8::BlockOnFence, IDirect3DDevice8::SetVerticalBlankCallback
